question: For each of these sequences find a recurrence relation satisfied by this sequence. (The answers are not unique because there are infinitely many different recurrence relations satisfied by any sequence.) a) a n = 𐓒 b) a n = 2n c) a n = 2n + 3 d) a n = 5 n
response: It looks like part (a) got garbled (“𐓒”), so I’ll handle (b)–(d) clearly, and you can resend (a) if needed.  ---  ### **(b) ( a_n = 2n )**  This is a linear sequence (increases by 2 each time).  A simple recurrence: [ a_n = a_{n-1} + 2,\quad \text{with } a_0 = 0 ]  ---  ### **(c) ( a_n = 2n + 3 )**  Also linear, same difference of 2.  A recurrence: [ a_n = a_{n-1} + 2,\quad \text{with } a_0 = 3 ]  ---  ### **(d) ( a_n = 5n )**  Again linear, difference is 5.  A recurrence: [ a_n = a_{n-1} + 5,\quad \text{with } a_0 = 0 ]  ---  ### General insight  All sequences of the form ( a_n = kn + c ) satisfy: [ a_n = a_{n-1} + k ]  ---  If you resend part (a), I’ll solve that one too.
